label: GCP Cloud SQL Postgres
description: Connect to a Google Cloud SQL PostgreSQL instance using a public IP or Cloud SQL Auth Proxy.

form.title: Connect GCP Cloud SQL Postgres
form.subtitle: Enter your Cloud SQL instance connection details to let Anton query your PostgreSQL database.

methods.label: Direct Connection (Public IP)
methods.description: Connect directly to your Cloud SQL instance using its public IP address, port, database name, and credentials. Quickest path if your instance has a public IP and your network allows access.
methods.how_to:
## Connect via Public IP

### Prerequisites
- Your Cloud SQL instance must have a **Public IP** enabled.
- Your client IP must be added to the instance's **Authorized networks** list.

### Steps

1. Open [Google Cloud Console](https://console.cloud.google.com/) and navigate to **Cloud SQL → Instances**.
2. Click your Postgres instance name to open its details page.
3. Go to the **Connections** tab → **Networking** section.
4. Under **Public IP**, confirm it is enabled. Copy the **Public IP address** shown.
5. If your machine's IP isn't already listed under **Authorized networks**, click **Add network**, enter your IP (e.g. `203.0.113.42/32`), and click **Done → Save**.
6. Note your **database name**, **database user**, and **password** (created under **Users** in the left sidebar or during instance setup).
7. Paste the public IP into the **Host** field below. Leave port as `5432` unless you changed it.
8. Toggle **Require SSL** if your instance enforces SSL (recommended for production).

### Notes
- Authorized networks changes can take up to 60 seconds to propagate.
- If you need to restrict access further, consider using the **Cloud SQL Auth Proxy** method instead.

fields.name: host
fields.label: Host
fields.description: Public IP address of your Cloud SQL instance. Find it in Cloud Console → Cloud SQL → Instances → your instance → Overview.

fields.name: port
fields.label: Port
fields.description: PostgreSQL port. Default is 5432 unless you configured a custom port.

fields.name: database
fields.label: Database
fields.description: Name of the PostgreSQL database to connect to.

fields.name: user
fields.label: Username
fields.description: Database user account. Find or create users under Cloud SQL → Instances → your instance → Users.

fields.name: ssl_enabled
fields.label: Require SSL
fields.description: Enable to enforce an encrypted SSL/TLS connection. Recommended for production instances. Check your instance's SSL settings under Connections → Security.

methods.label: Cloud SQL Auth Proxy
methods.description: Connect via the Cloud SQL Auth Proxy running locally. The proxy handles IAM-based authentication and encrypts traffic without needing to add authorized networks or manage SSL certificates manually.
methods.how_to:
## Connect via Cloud SQL Auth Proxy

The Cloud SQL Auth Proxy creates a secure tunnel from your machine to Cloud SQL using IAM credentials, so you don't need to whitelist IPs or manage SSL certificates.

### Prerequisites
- [gcloud CLI](https://cloud.google.com/sdk/docs/install) installed and authenticated (`gcloud auth application-default login`).
- The proxy binary downloaded and running.

### Steps

1. Open [Google Cloud Console](https://console.cloud.google.com/) → **Cloud SQL → Instances**.
2. Click your instance and copy the **Connection name** from the Overview page. It looks like `project-id:region:instance-id`.
3. Download the [Cloud SQL Auth Proxy](https://cloud.google.com/sql/docs/postgres/sql-proxy#install) for your OS.
4. Start the proxy in a terminal, binding to a local port:
   ```
   ./cloud-sql-proxy --port 5432 project-id:region:instance-id
   ```
5. The proxy listens on `127.0.0.1:5432`. Use that as the **Host** and **Port** below.
6. Enter your **database name**, **username**, and **password** as normal.
7. SSL can be left off (`false`) since the proxy handles encryption internally.

### Notes
- The proxy process must be running whenever Anton connects. Consider running it as a background service.
- Your gcloud account needs the **Cloud SQL Client** IAM role on the project.
- See [Google's proxy documentation](https://cloud.google.com/sql/docs/postgres/sql-proxy) for service-account key authentication if you don't want to use user credentials.

fields.name: host
fields.label: Proxy Host
fields.description: Hostname where the Cloud SQL Auth Proxy is listening. Typically 127.0.0.1 when running locally.

fields.name: port
fields.label: Port
fields.description: Local port the Cloud SQL Auth Proxy is bound to (set with --port when starting the proxy).

fields.name: database
fields.label: Database
fields.description: Name of the PostgreSQL database to connect to.

fields.name: user
fields.label: Username
fields.description: Database user account. Manage users under Cloud SQL → Instances → your instance → Users.

fields.name: ssl_enabled
fields.label: Require SSL
fields.description: The Auth Proxy encrypts traffic automatically. Leave off unless your DBA has configured additional SSL requirements at the database level.

methods.label: Private IP (VPC)
methods.description: Connect using the instance's private VPC IP. Suitable when Anton runs inside the same VPC or a peered network, with no public internet exposure.
methods.how_to:
## Connect via Private IP

Use this method when Anton is running inside the same Google Cloud VPC (or a VPC-peered network) as your Cloud SQL instance.

### Prerequisites
- Your Cloud SQL instance must have **Private IP** enabled.
- Anton's host must be on the same VPC or a peered network reachable from it.

### Steps

1. Open [Google Cloud Console](https://console.cloud.google.com/) → **Cloud SQL → Instances**.
2. Click your instance → **Connections** tab → **Networking**.
3. Confirm **Private IP** is enabled and note the private IP address shown (e.g. `10.0.0.5`).
4. If Private IP isn't enabled, click **Edit instance → Connections → Private IP**, select the VPC network, and save. This may take a few minutes.
5. Paste the private IP into **Host** below. Use port `5432` (default).
6. Enter your database name, username, and password.
7. Enable **Require SSL** if your instance enforces SSL (check under **Connections → Security**).

### Notes
- Private IP connectivity requires VPC peering between your host's network and the Cloud SQL service network, which is set up automatically when you enable Private IP.
- Firewall rules on the VPC must allow TCP on port 5432 from Anton's host IP.

fields.name: host
fields.label: Private IP Host
fields.description: Private VPC IP of your Cloud SQL instance. Found under Cloud SQL → Instances → your instance → Connections → Networking → Private IP.

fields.name: database
fields.label: Database
fields.description: Name of the PostgreSQL database to connect to.

fields.name: ssl_enabled
fields.label: Require SSL
fields.description: Enable SSL encryption. Recommended even on private networks. Check your instance's SSL mode under Connections → Security.
